status.headline: 🎉 SWO RESCINDED — April 10, 2026. 52 days of hell, officially over.

nextAction.title: 🎉 SWO Rescinded — Construction Restarts Monday April 13
nextAction.detail: Damian confirmed at 3:39 PM: SWO for construction and electrical WWP has been rescinded. DOB BIS shows complaint #3931133 status RES (Resolved), disposition L1. 52 days from issuance to rescission.

keyDates.label: April 10 — ✅ PASSED
keyDates.desc: 🎉 SWO Re-Inspection PASSED. Lenny Vicari inspected 176 Johnson St. SWO formally rescinded. Complaint #3931133 → RES (Resolved), disposition L1.

keyDates.label: April 18 — ✅ MET
keyDates.desc: 60-day COC filing deadline (§28-219.1). Both violations ARE Class 1 (confirmed BIS Apr 8). §28-219.1 applies, but penalty triggers on failure to FILE — COC filed April 3, within window. Deadline met.

keyDates.label: May 1–8 (est.)
keyDates.desc: Expected COC review completion. 4–5 weeks from April 3 submission. Can be accelerated in person at 280 Broadway.

keyDates.label: May 6 — 10:30 AM
keyDates.desc: ECB Hearing: Electrical violation 39177537R (resolved, but hearing still scheduled)

keyDates.label: May 31
keyDates.desc: Work permit #429322759 expires. SWO lifted — permit now active.

keyDates.label: April 13 — 🏗️ RESTART
keyDates.desc: Construction resumes. Pawel targeting Monday April 13 as restart date.

penalties.name: 60-Day Cure Window
penalties.detail: ✅ Met — COC filed April 3. Both violations ARE Class 1, §28-219.1 applies, but penalty is for failure to FILE (not approve). Filing deadline met.

rent.description: $500/week since first week of March. Should have moved in March 1 — SWO made that impossible.

blockers.name: ✅ Civil penalties — BOTH CONFIRMED PAID
blockers.detail: GC CP (39177526L, $15K) confirmed paid Mar 27 via CP_GC.pdf. Electrical CP (39177537R) confirmed paid by Damian Apr 8 email.

blockers.name: ✅ SWO rescission inspection — PASSED
blockers.detail: April 10, 2026. Lenny Vicari inspected. SWO formally rescinded. Complaint #3931133 → RES (Resolved), disposition L1.

blockers.name: ✅ SWO RESCINDED — Construction can resume
blockers.detail: Damian confirmed 3:39 PM April 10. DOB BIS updated. 52 days from issuance to rescission.

blockers.name: Second COC approval (39177526L)
blockers.detail: Still in AEU review (submitted April 3, ~4–5 weeks). NOT required for SWO rescission (confirmed — SWO lifted without it). Still needed for May 6 OATH hearing.

blockers.name: May 6 OATH hearing (39177526L)
blockers.detail: ECB hearing still scheduled. COC approval before this date would strengthen position.

timelineDone.desc: Partial Stop Work Order issued. Two violations: electrical (39177537R) and construction (39177526L). 60-day cure filing window starts.
First work permit issued for 6A renovation.
SWO cleared for commercial space (not 6A).
Allan meets with board members. Learns Jack Jaffa is an expeditor (not a law firm). ECB hearings are optional.
Electrical corrective work completed in 6A (BX wiring for lighting, receptacles, switches).
First Certificate of Correction filed for electrical violation (COC0393020).
First request for SWO rescission inspection sent to DOB.
First work permit expires (no work possible under SWO anyway).
SWO rescission follow-up #2 sent to DOB.
Damian update: electrical CP paid, GC civil penalty still waiting on DOB for amount.
SWO rescission follow-up #3 sent to DOB. GC CP request now 13 business days with no response.
$15,000 electrical civil penalty paid via certified check to NYC DOB.
Second COC filed for construction violation (COC0395691) — summons 39177526L.
Allan asks attorney: "Can 6A resume after just 6A SWO rescind, or must ALL SWOs clear?"
First COC approved. Electrical violation 39177537R = RESOLVED in BIS.
Damian: "Great! I will take care of the other one as well."
DOB asks Damian to send permits/applications/fees PDFs to OBM before scheduling rescission inspection.
New work permit issued (#429322759, expires May 31). Cannot use until SWO lifted.
Second COC actually submitted to AEU (by Agnieszka Muczynski, not Damian).
Damian confirms docs sent to OBM for rescission inspection.
Full status check-in email sent to entire team.
Attorney Eric Wohl: "I can't speak to SWO removals. That's an expeditor question."
Allan finds on DOB NOW: GC civil penalty review (670218027) shows APPROVED.
Pawel speaks with Damian. Damian will follow up with DOB. Does NOT answer COC or penalty questions.
Allan calls AEU x3. COC pending. Normal review: 4–5 weeks. Owner can go to 280 Broadway in person. Expediter CANNOT go.
Allan calls OBM. They don't see April 18 deadline — only May 6 hearing. (Confirms April 18 is cure window, not penalty cliff.)
Allan emails OBM supervisor re: SWO rescission requirements.
Brooklyn Construction Enforcement: "We have nothing to do with this." OBM is the only office for SWO rescission.
Lenny Vicari (OBM Supervising Inspector) replies in 14 minutes — offers re-inspection Friday April 10, 8–10 AM.
Allan confirms Friday April 10, 8–10 AM. Pawel on-site (confirmed).

Damian email (2:33 PM): Electrical CP (EL WWP) confirmed paid with filing fee. Payment proof attached to SWO rescind request — sent to Electrical SWO office and OBM. Both CPs now confirmed paid. Financial blockers for SWO rescission cleared.
Damian (6:17 AM): Person on-site during inspection should ask the DOB inspector for the formal rescind order. DOB usually removes red banner from property profile same day, but can take longer.
Pawel (7:05 AM): "I will be there and will ask." — Pawel confirmed for inspection and briefed to request formal rescind order from inspector.
Eugene (6:09 AM): Sent Pawel stamped DOBNOW drawings before inspection window.
Pawel (3:03 PM): "All good." Inspection went well. Damian checking DOB system for SWO removal.

🎉 Damian (3:39 PM): "The SWO for the construction and electrical WWP has been rescinded." DOB BIS: Complaint #3931133 → Status RES (Resolved), Disposition L1. SWO officially lifted. 52 days of hell, over.

timelineTodo.desc: Second COC approved by AEU (COC0395691 for 39177526L). Not required for SWO but needed for May 6 OATH hearing.
May 6 OATH hearing — ECB hearings for both violations.

flowchart.text: File Certificate of Correction (COC) with AEU

flowchart.note: Or go in person to 280 Broadway (owner only, deed + ID)

flowchart.text: Submit docs to OBM for SWO rescission

survivalGuide.keyInsight: COC approval and SWO rescission ARE independent tracks. We got our SWO rescinded on April 10 without the second COC being approved. Push both simultaneously — don't wait for one to complete the other.

contacts.name: AEU (Administrative Enforcement Unit)
contacts.desc: COC review status, civil penalty status

contacts.name: Lenny Vicari — Supervising Inspector (OBM)
contacts.desc: Direct contact for SWO rescission inspection at 176 Johnson St

contacts.name: Brooklyn Construction Enforcement
contacts.desc: ❌ Dead end for SWO — referred us to OBM

tips.title: Your expediter can't do everything.
tips.body: Two out of three AEU reps told me only the owner can go to 280 Broadway in person to speed up a COC review. Bring your deed and ID. Don't assume your expediter is handling it — verify.

tips.title: Call multiple times.
tips.body: I called AEU three times and got different info each time. One rep said my expediter could come. Two said only the owner. Triangulate.

tips.title: COC and SWO may be separate tracks.
tips.body: AEU reviews your COC. OBM handles the SWO. One rep told me they don't think COC approval is needed before the SWO can be lifted. Push both simultaneously.

tips.title: The 60-day deadline IS for filing, not approval — and we met it.
tips.body: Per 1 RCNY §102-01, the 60-day 'cure' window is about filing the COC (done April 3). The $5K+$1K/day penalty we initially feared comes from §28-219.1, which only applies to Class 1 (immediately hazardous) violations — not our Class 2 WWP. Neither AEU nor OBM flagged April 18 as a crisis. May 6 OATH hearing is the real action date.

tips.title: DOB gets frustrated with too many follow-ups.
tips.body: My contractor warned me: expediters can push to a point, then DOB pushes back. Balance persistence with patience.

tips.title: Check DOB NOW yourself.
tips.body: Don't wait for your expediter. Go to the DOB NOW portal and look up your property. I found my civil penalty review was approved before anyone told me.

tips.title: Go to 280 Broadway.
tips.body: If your COC review is taking too long, go in person. 280 Broadway, lower Manhattan, 8:30–3:45 weekdays. Deed + government ID. Highest-impact move an owner can make.

tips.title: Document everything.
tips.body: Every call, text, email. I built this entire site because it's the only way to track what's happening across 10+ people and 3 government agencies.

tips.title: Know AEU vs. OBM vs. ECB.
tips.body: AEU reviews your Certificate of Correction. OBM handles SWO rescission and inspections. ECB handles hearings and penalty adjudication. They're separate.

tips.title: Jack Jaffa is an expeditor, not a law firm.
tips.body: Despite the name, Jack Jaffa & Associates handles DOB paperwork. Legal defense is separate.

openQuestions.q: ✅ RESOLVED: Have both civil penalties been paid?
openQuestions.why: GC CP confirmed via CP_GC.pdf (Apr 8). Electrical CP confirmed by Damian email (Apr 8, 2:33 PM). Both paid. Financial requirement for SWO rescission (§ 28-207.2.3) met.
§ 28-207.2.6 — $6K–$12K per violation. Must be paid before rescission. Unknown if any were issued.

openQuestions.q: ✅ RESOLVED: Does the COC for 39177526L need to be approved before SWO can be rescinded?
openQuestions.why: CONFIRMED NO — SWO rescinded April 10 without COC approval. COC still in AEU review. Independent tracks confirmed.

openQuestions.q: ✅ RESOLVED: Can 6A resume after just 6A SWO rescind?
openQuestions.why: YES — SWO rescinded for 6A on April 10. Construction restarts Monday April 13.
She submitted the COC on April 3, not Damian. Is she at Big Orange? Does this affect anything?

intelLog.intel: "We have nothing to do with this." SWO rescission is exclusively OBM.
intelLog.impact: Eliminates Brooklyn CE as a path. OBM is the only office.

intelLog.intel: Responded to email in 14 minutes. Offered re-inspection Friday April 10. Did NOT mention needing COC approval first.
intelLog.impact: Strongest signal yet that COC and SWO are independent tracks.

intelLog.intel: COC review is 4–5 weeks. Owner can go to 280 Broadway to push. Expediters CANNOT go in person.
intelLog.impact: 280 Broadway trip is owner-only. But may not be needed if SWO can be lifted without COC.

intelLog.intel: April 18 'deadline' is actually the 60-day cure FILING window — already met (COC filed Apr 3). The $5K+$1K/day penalty is from §28-219.1, which applies only to Class 1 (immediately hazardous) violations. Our violation (39177526L) is Class 2. That penalty does NOT apply.
intelLog.impact: April 18 is no longer a risk. May 6 OATH hearing is the real action date. 280 Broadway trip is about getting COC approved before May 6, not avoiding a penalty cliff.

intelLog.intel: "The CP for EL WWP was paid along with the filing fee. I attached the payment confirmation to my SWO rescind request and sent it to the Electrical SWO and then to the OBM."
intelLog.impact: Electrical CP confirmed paid. Damian has already filed SWO rescind request with OBM, payment proof attached. Both civil penalties now confirmed paid — financial blockers cleared.

intelLog.source: Damian (Big Orange) email — 6:17 AM + Pawel email — 7:05 AM

intelLog.impact: Pawel is coordinated and ready. The key action for tomorrow is Pawel explicitly requesting the formal rescind order from Lenny during the inspection.

intelLog.intel: "All good." Inspection went well. Damian checking DOB system for SWO removal.
intelLog.impact: Inspection passed. Awaiting DOB system update.

intelLog.intel: "The SWO for the construction and electrical WWP has been rescinded." Attached DOB BIS screenshot: Complaint #3931133, Status RES (Resolved), Disposition L1.
intelLog.impact: 🎉 SWO officially rescinded. 52 days from issuance to resolution. Construction can resume Monday April 13.

intelLog.intel: Sent Pawel stamped DOBNOW drawings before inspection window.
GC civil penalty review (670218027) shows APPROVED. But 'approved' may mean review approved, not payment confirmed.

legalRequirements.title: NYC Admin Code Requirements for SWO Rescission

sections.text: All civil penalties must be PAID before SWO rescission.
sections.status: ✅ MET — Both CPs confirmed paid (GC: Mar 27, Electrical: confirmed Apr 8 by Damian)

sections.text: Work-against-SWO penalties ($6K–$12K each) must also be paid.
Commissioner must inspect and confirm conditions corrected.
